events.title: Eastern Market — Weekend Market & Flea
events.description: Historic public market plus weekend outdoor flea, arts, and crafts. Live music when weather permits.

events.title: Capitol Hill Farmers Markets
events.description: Seasonal weekend farmers markets with local produce and vendors.

events.title: Lincoln Park
events.description: Large Capitol Hill green space with playground, paths, and statues. Dog-friendly. Cafes at both ends.

events.title: Garfield Park
events.description: Neighborhood park with playground, tennis, basketball, bocce, and a community garden.

events.title: Yards Park & Navy Yard Waterfront
events.description: Riverfront park with fountains, boardwalk, and frequent seasonal weekend programming.

events.title: National Mall & Smithsonian Museums
events.description: Free Smithsonian museums and monuments. Reliable all-weather, all-ages option.

events.title: United States Botanic Garden
events.description: Free conservatory and gardens at the foot of the Capitol, with a Children's Garden.

events.title: Anacostia Riverwalk Trail Ride
events.description: Paved riverfront trail for a family bike ride, with Capital Bikeshare access nearby.

events.title: The Fields at RFK — Community Open Play & Disc Golf
events.time: Open hours vary (8 AM–dusk disc golf)
events.venue: The Fields at RFK Campus (401 Oklahoma Ave NE)
events.description: Turf fields with community open-play hours plus DC's free disc golf course. 10-min walk from Stadium-Armory.

events.title: CHAW Gallery (Capitol Hill Arts Workshop)
events.venue: Capitol Hill Arts Workshop (545 7th St SE)
events.description: Rotating gallery shows of local artists, two blocks from Eastern Market. Check site for current exhibition.

events.title: National STEM Festival
events.venue: National Mall and partner institutions (Smithsonian Air & Space Museum, National Academy of Sciences)
events.description: Free public festival powered by EXPLR bringing together the nation's top young minds. Hands-on celebration of youth innovation across AI, aerospace, and more. Features robotics competitions, interactive exhibits, and panel discussions with astronauts, engineers, and technologists.

events.title: 34th Annual Giant National Capital BBQ Battle
events.time: Sat 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Sun 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM
events.venue: Pennsylvania Avenue NW between 3rd and 7th Streets NW
events.price: Free (visit Giant stores for free admission offer; tickets also at bbqdc.com)
events.description: America's official National BBQ Championship returns to Pennsylvania Avenue between the Capitol and the National Archives. Features world-class BBQ, international flavors, celebrity chefs, 6 stages of live music (rock, reggae, salsa, R&B, go-go), eating contests, and family activities. Part of DC250 celebrations. Draws 100,000+ attendees annually.

events.title: A Fine Madness
events.venue: Woolly Mammoth Theatre + partner sites (Dupont Underground, Hamiltonian Artists, The Nicholson Project)
events.price: Check woollymammoth.net; Woolly offers pay-what-you-can and $20 under-30 pricing
events.description: Created and performed by Woolly company member Justin Weaks, A Fine Madness is a bold, deeply personal one-person show that transforms theater into a living archive. Performances tour across multiple DC venues from Woolly's Rehearsal Hall to Dupont Underground, Hamiltonian Artists, and The Nicholson Project.

events.title: Fest Wknd – R&B & Soca Block Party
events.description: Multi-day R&B and soca block party at Navy Yard. Each day has its own theme — live R&B hits, hip-swaying soca, '00s throwbacks, and more — with six artists and 12 DJs over three days.

events.title: Suffs
events.venue: The National Theatre, 1321 Pennsylvania Ave NW
events.price: Broadway touring prices; avg ~$200+. TodayTix rush/lottery likely available.
events.description: The Tony Award-winning musical about the women's suffrage movement — 'Behind every powerful woman… are more powerful women.' National Broadway tour stop at The National Theatre for a two-week limited engagement.

events.title: Jazz in the Garden — National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden
events.venue: National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden
events.description: A beloved free outdoor jazz concert series at the National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden, running all summer on Friday evenings from 5–8:30pm. Bring a blanket, stake out a spot on the grass, and enjoy live jazz among the sculptures. Food and drinks available for purchase. Note: June 27 and July 3 Fridays fall within the target weekends. Check NGA's site for the specific lineup each week.

events.title: Eastern Market – Weekend Farmers & Flea Market
events.description: Historic public market open Tuesday–Sunday with local farmers, artisans, and crafters. Weekend outdoor flea and arts markets expand the footprint along the Capitol Hill streets. Free to browse; open across all three target weekends.

events.title: How Shakespeare Saved My Life
events.venue: Folger Shakespeare Library, 201 East Capitol St SE
events.price: Tickets from $20; 50% discount for public servants with code THANKYOU
events.description: World premiere solo show written and performed by Broadway star Jacob Ming-Trent, directed by Tony Taccone. An autobiographical dive into the playwright's turbulent youth, blending Shakespeare with the voices of Basquiat, Biggie, and Tupac. Co-produced by Berkeley Repertory Theatre, Folger Theatre, and Red Bull Theater. Run time: 90 minutes.

events.title: Fierce Flora: Tales of Survival and Demise (Exhibit)
events.description: A free USBG exhibit exploring the unique and sometimes alarming survival strategies plants have evolved — carnivorous plants, toxic defenses, and more. Highly engaging for curious teens and preteens. Open daily during regular USBG hours alongside the America's State Flowers exhibit.

events.title: Smithsonian American Art Museum & National Portrait Gallery — Free Admission & Kogod Courtyard
events.venue: Smithsonian American Art Museum / National Portrait Gallery (8th & F St NW)
events.description: Two museums in one building, free and open until 7pm — ideal for summer afternoon visits. The building's Kogod Courtyard hosts free performances including the monthly Take 5: Jazz at SAAM series. 'American Aspirations,' a major exhibition bringing together iconic Smithsonian objects to commemorate the nation's founding, is on view June 2–July 26, 2026.

events.title: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History — Free General Admission
events.venue: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
events.description: All Smithsonian museums on the National Mall are free and open daily. The National Museum of Natural History is a perennial favorite for teens and families, featuring the Hope Diamond, ocean and mammal halls, dinosaurs, and the Q?rius hands-on science education center. The museum's new 'From These Lands' exhibition opened June 2026.

events.title: USBG Children's Garden — Open Exploration
events.venue: United States Botanic Garden — Children's Garden
events.description: The USBG Children's Garden is open throughout summer 2026, inviting families to explore, dig, plant, water, and engage hands-on with the garden environment. A relaxed drop-in experience that pairs well with the America's State Flowers exhibit. Free and no tickets required.

events.title: Great American State Fair (DC250 Semiquincentennial Exposition)
events.venue: National Mall (U.S. Capitol to Washington Monument)
events.description: A 16-day national exposition spanning the National Mall as part of America's 250th anniversary. Showcases all 56 U.S. states and territories in a World's Fair-scale event with live music, carnival rides, and dozens of hands-on partner activations. Covers the June 27 and July 4 target weekends.

events.title: Great American State Fair on the National Mall
events.venue: National Mall (U.S. Capitol to Washington Monument)
events.description: A 16-day national exposition spanning the National Mall as part of DC250/Freedom 250 celebrations. Features a 110-foot giant Ferris Wheel with panoramic views from the Capitol to the Lincoln Memorial (free, timed reservation required), a restored 1947 Smithsonian carousel, cultural performances, and concerts between the Smithsonian Castle and the Hirshhorn Museum.

events.title: Great American State Fair on the National Mall
events.description: A massive free 16-day fair stretching the full length of the National Mall, featuring food, entertainment, and cultural showcases from all 50 U.S. states and territories. Highlights include a 110-foot Ferris wheel. Active across the June 27 and July 4 target weekends. Overlaps with the FIFA World Cup Fan Zone for a packed National Mall experience.

events.title: Feeling Afraid As If Something Terrible Is Going to Happen
events.price: Studio Theatre tickets typically $45–$95; under-30 and rush pricing available
events.description: A new play running at Studio Theatre through mid-July 2026. Studio Theatre is known for intimate, thought-provoking productions. Under-30 and TodayTix rush tickets are typically available for Studio shows.

events.title: Beetlejuice
events.venue: The National Theatre, 1321 Pennsylvania Ave NW
events.price: Broadway touring prices; avg ~$200+. TodayTix rush/lottery likely available.
events.description: IT'S SHOWTIME! The wildly popular Broadway musical comedy based on the Tim Burton film returns on national tour. Beetlejuice is a musical comedy about making the most of every day-o — ghosts, the afterlife, and plenty of laughs.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Fan Zone on the National Mall
events.time: Gates open 1 hour before first match; close 1 hour after last match (up to midnight July 4)
events.description: A free, all-ages FIFA World Cup Fan Zone on the National Mall running the full length of the 2026 tournament (June 11–July 19), including all three target weekends. Features a giant Jumbotron showing live match broadcasts — all USA and all knockout-round games shown regardless of time. Also includes youth programming, cultural showcases, interactive exhibits, food, and music. On July 4 the zone stays open until midnight. Pre-registration encouraged but walk-ups welcome; security screening required.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Fan Zone on the National Mall
events.time: Opens 1 hour before first match; closes 1 hour after last match
events.venue: National Mall, between 3rd and 4th Street NW
events.description: Official Freedom 250 FIFA World Cup Fan Zone on the National Mall, running June 11–July 19 and covering all three target weekends. Features live match viewing on a giant screen, interactive exhibits, youth soccer programming, cultural showcases, food, music, and family-friendly activations. All USA matches shown; knockout-round games (starting June 28) shown regardless of time.

events.title: American Aspirations (Exhibition at Smithsonian Castle)
events.description: A free Smithsonian exhibition running June 2–July 26, 2026 at the iconic Castle building on the National Mall. Brings together some of the Smithsonian's most treasured objects to commemorate America's founding — timed perfectly with the America250 celebrations. Spans all three target weekends.

events.title: Pippin
events.venue: Signature Theatre (The Max), 4200 Campbell Ave, Arlington, VA
events.price: Signature single tickets typically $40–$100+; rush and under-30 pricing may apply
events.description: Stephen Schwartz and Roger O. Hirson's beloved musical follows young Pippin on his search for meaning, magic, and his 'corner of the sky.' Signature Theatre's production runs through late July 2026 in Arlington, VA.

events.title: CrazySexyCool – The TLC Musical
events.price: Tickets from ~$45; TodayTix rush/lottery may apply
events.description: A musical celebrating the iconic R&B group TLC, tracing the rise of Tionne 'T-Boz' Watkins, Lisa 'Left Eye' Lopes, and Rozonda 'Chilli' Thomas from scrappy newcomers to one of the best-selling female groups of all time.

events.title: The Fruits of Our Labor
events.venue: Arena Stage (Fichandler Stage / Kreeger Theatre), 1101 6th St SW
events.price: Tickets from ~$45; TodayTix rush/lottery may apply
events.description: A world-premiere production from Step Afrika! commemorating America's 250th anniversary. Directed by Jakari Sherman, this dance-theater work blends step performance with storytelling to celebrate the labor and legacy that built the nation.

events.title: Discover Summer for Teens — Know Your Power Art Contest
events.venue: DC Public Library (all branches / online submission)
events.description: DCPL's teen-specific Discover Summer track invites youth ages 13–19 to submit an original work of writing, photography, film, visual art, or music that expresses their perspective on a social issue. Alongside the contest, teens can drop into creative workshops in music, visual arts, and writing at branches throughout the summer.

events.title: Discover Summer — All-Ages Reading & Learning Program
events.time: Varies by branch; most branches open Sat 10am–5pm, select Sun hours
events.venue: All DC Public Library Branches (anchor: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library)
events.description: DC Public Library's flagship summer program running June 1–Aug. 31 across all branches. Families, kids, teens, and adults complete activity-based bingo challenges, attend free events, and earn prizes including Washington Nationals tickets. Over 2,600 free events system-wide. Pick up a Discover Summer Bingo Board at any neighborhood library or register via Beanstack.

events.title: Voices and Votes: Exploring Democracy Across America
events.venue: Smithsonian Arts and Industries Building, 900 Jefferson Drive SW
events.description: The Smithsonian Arts and Industries Building reopens to present 'Voices and Votes,' an exhibit exploring what American independence really means and how that meaning has evolved from the American Revolution to the present day. Located in the Smithsonian's second-oldest building.

events.title: District Vibes / American Pride: How D.C. Changed American Culture (Exhibit)
events.time: During library open hours (Mon–Thu 9am–9pm, Fri–Sat 9:30am–5:30pm, Sun 1–5pm)
events.venue: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library — Central Library
events.description: A free public exhibit at the MLK Jr. Memorial Library exploring how Washington, D.C. shaped American culture. On view June 3–September 27, 2026 — fully spanning all three target weekends. Part of the library's broader Art & Exhibits program, which uses exhibitions as a portal to community engagement.

events.title: America's State Flowers: An America250 Celebration (Exhibit)
events.venue: United States Botanic Garden Conservatory & Outdoor Gardens
events.description: A sweeping free exhibit at the US Botanic Garden (through Oct. 12, 2026) showcasing the official flowers of all 56 U.S. states and territories in living plants, botanical illustrations, herbarium specimens, and intricate paper sculptures by artist Emily Paluska. Drop-in demonstrations and educational programs are offered throughout the run. Children's Garden also open for hands-on digging, planting, and exploration. No tickets required.

events.title: Revelation: A Journey Into Abstraction (NMAAHC)
events.venue: National Museum of African American History and Culture, 1400 Constitution Ave NW
events.description: NMAAHC dives into abstraction with this installation exploring the profound range and depth of abstract art created by African American artists from the mid-20th century to the present. Reintroduces key works from the museum's collection and debuts new acquisitions.

events.title: Capital Pride Parade
events.description: The annual Capital Pride Parade, one of DC's largest LGBTQ+ celebrations, marches through downtown. Exhibitors and performers parade through DC to show their spirit. The festival stage is on Pennsylvania Avenue between 3rd and 7th Streets NW.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Patrice DeVeaux, Shaun Murphy & More
events.description: Weekend showcase headlined by Patrice DeVeaux, with Shaun Murphy, Christian Escoto, and Joey Duffield. Part of the Jun 19–21 run at DC's premier comedy club. Approximately 90 minutes.

events.title: Everyday People Day Party at The Bullpen
events.description: Groovy outdoor DJ day party at The Bullpen in Navy Yard, featuring R&B, hip-hop, and more genres.

events.title: Fête de la Musique Georgetown
events.description: Georgetown hosts several live acts and concerts as an ode to the French holiday Fête de la Musique, a worldwide celebration of music on the summer solstice.

events.title: FIFA World Cup Watch Party – Tingey Plaza (USA vs. Australia weekend)
events.description: D.C. United hosts free FIFA World Cup 2026 watch parties at Tingey Plaza in Navy Yard the weekend of June 19–21, including USA vs. Australia on June 19. Sign up for free admission tickets online.

events.title: Home Rule Music Festival
events.venue: The Great Lawn, Walter Reed Campus, 1301 Main Drive NW
events.description: DC-centric festival celebrating the city's rich music and culture. Lineup includes Sun Ra Arkestra featuring Marshall Allen, Chuck Brown Band, Trouble Funk, and Melanie Charles. Features live music across genres (jazz, go-go, soul, hip-hop), a Music & Record Fair with local record shops, live visual art, mural painting, a Kids Zone, local food vendors, and a Youth Showcase where student musicians open the festival.

events.title: Navy Yard FIFA World Cup 2026 Watch Party Weekend (United in Play)
events.venue: Tingey Plaza SE (101 Tingey St SE), Navy Yard
events.description: Part of a three-day official FIFA World Cup Watch Party (June 19–21) at Tingey Plaza in Navy Yard, in partnership with DC United's United in Play Soccer Celebration. Free community event with match screenings and neighborhood festivities. June 20 is the Saturday date of this weekend run.

events.title: Tim Meadows Live – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse
events.description: Emmy-winning SNL alumnus Tim Meadows performs at the Arlington Drafthouse. Part of a Jun 18–20 run at this art-deco dine-in comedy theater. Full bar/restaurant menu served at your seat.

events.title: Washington Blade Pride on the Pier (8th Annual)
events.description: Now in its 8th year, the Washington Blade's Pride on the Pier extends the city's Capital Pride celebration to The Wharf waterfront with a full day of live music, dancing, and LGBTQ+ community programming.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Patrice DeVeaux, Shaun Murphy & More (Sunday)
events.description: Final night of the Jun 19–21 showcase run featuring Patrice DeVeaux, Shaun Murphy, Christian Escoto, and Joey Duffield at DC's most famous comedy stage.

events.title: Flawed Mangoes: The Killswitch Memories Tour
events.description: Flawed Mangoes bring their Killswitch Memories Tour to Union Stage on Sunday evening.

events.title: Dauood Naimyar – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse (Friday)
events.description: Stand-up comedian Dauood Naimyar headlines at Arlington's dine-in comedy theater. Part of a multi-show run at the Drafthouse.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Joey Friedman, Chris Alan, Matt Deakins & More (Friday)
events.description: Weekend showcase featuring Joey Friedman, Chris Alan, Matt Deakins, Caitlin McDevitt, and Matt Brown. Part of the Jun 25–26 run.

events.title: Navy Yard Friday Night Concert Series – Hi Fi! Soul Band
events.venue: Yards Park Boardwalk, 355 Water Street SE
events.description: Free outdoor concert on the Yards Park Boardwalk featuring Hi Fi! Soul Band, performing a wide-ranging soul and R&B set covering Beyoncé, Stevie Wonder, Earth Wind & Fire, Bruno Mars, and more. Family-friendly with open grassy space along the Anacostia riverfront.

events.title: Carmen Lynch – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse
events.description: Carmen Lynch — known for appearances on The Tonight Show, The Late Show with Colbert, and Letterman — headlines at Arlington's dine-in comedy theater. Multi-show run over the Jun 27 weekend.

events.title: Tara Cannistraci – DC Comedy Loft
events.description: Stand-up headliner Tara Cannistraci performs at the DC Comedy Loft, located at 1523 22nd St NW. Multi-show weekend engagement.

events.title: The Illharmonic – Black Music Month
events.description: The Illharmonic combines Hip-Hop with orchestral accompaniment for a Black Music Month performance at Lincoln Theatre.

events.title: Himra
events.description: Himra performs at Union Stage on Sunday evening.

events.title: Michael Henry & Tim Murray – DC Comedy Loft (Sunday)
events.description: Comedian duo Michael Henry & Tim Murray headline the DC Comedy Loft on Sunday. Multi-show format at this intimate West End venue.

events.title: Tony Woods – DC Improv
events.description: Veteran stand-up comedian Tony Woods performs at DC Improv on Sunday evening. One-night engagement.

events.title: Navy Yard Friday Night Concert Series – SALTY (July 3 Release Party)
events.venue: Yards Park Boardwalk, 355 Water Street SE
events.description: Free outdoor concert on the Yards Park Boardwalk featuring SALTY in a high-energy waterfront performance that doubles as the official release party for their new single 'Ibiza Skies.' Includes an exclusive vinyl giveaway for fans. Family-friendly along the Anacostia riverfront.

events.title: Rock the Dock Free Summer Concert – Country Line Dance Night (DC Rawhides & Shawn Parsons)
events.venue: Transit Pier at The Wharf (970 Wharf Street SW)
events.description: Free Friday night outdoor concert on the Transit Pier featuring a Country Line Dance Night with DC Rawhides and live music by Shawn Parsons & The Secret Service. Pre-Fourth of July waterfront celebration. Food from any Wharf restaurant permitted.

events.title: A Capitol Fourth Concert
events.venue: West Lawn, U.S. Capitol, Capitol Hill
events.description: The iconic 90-minute nationally broadcast Independence Day concert on the West Lawn of the U.S. Capitol, featuring the National Symphony Orchestra, major vocalists, and military bands. Part of the America 250th semiquincentennial celebration. Bring blankets and low chairs; the lawn opens in the late afternoon.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Patrice DeVeaux, Ross Benoit, Justo & More (July 4th Weekend)
events.description: Holiday weekend showcase at DC Improv featuring Patrice DeVeaux, Ross Benoit, Justo, Jack Coleman, and David Balagizi. Jul 3 lineup confirmed — check for Jul 4–5 continuation shows.

events.title: Fourth at The Wharf – VIP Experience & Fireworks
events.price: VIP ticketed; some public areas free
events.description: Independence Day celebration at The Wharf waterfront featuring live music, fireworks over the Potomac, and a VIP experience package. The Wharf hosts its annual Fourth of July programming with boat parades and waterfront festivities for America's 250th anniversary.

events.title: Fourth of July Fireworks on the National Mall
events.venue: National Mall (launched from Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool)
events.description: America's 250th birthday fireworks display over the National Mall, billed as a world-record attempt in scale. Shells launched from platforms flanking the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Free entry at the Freedom 250 event area (14th–17th Streets on the Mall); pre-registration encouraged but walk-ups welcome. Expect massive crowds — organizers anticipate over one million visitors. Metro strongly recommended.

events.title: National Archives July 4th Celebration
events.description: The only place where you can view the original Declaration of Independence hosts a special July 4th party on America's 250th anniversary. Features George and Martha Washington re-enactors, family-fun activities, and a chance to sign a facsimile of the Declaration on the steps of the Archives.

events.title: National Independence Day Parade
events.description: America's 250th anniversary Independence Day Parade — part of DC250/Freedom 250 — runs along Constitution Avenue. Organizers promise an expanded parade with elaborate floats, costumes, and performances. Designated a National Special Security Event; expect security screening and large crowds. Metro strongly recommended.

events.title: Front Royal
events.description: Front Royal plays Pearl Street Warehouse on the Sunday of July 4th weekend. Free RSVP event, all ages.

events.title: Andrew Orolfo – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse (Friday)
events.description: Stand-up comedian Andrew Orolfo headlines at the Arlington Drafthouse. Part of a multi-show run at this dine-in comedy theater in Arlington, VA.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Stacey Axler, Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman & More (Saturday)
events.description: Weekend showcase headlined by Stacey Axler with Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman, and Ty Davis. Part of the Jul 9–12 run at DC Improv.

events.title: Escape with Washington Improv Theater – Saturday Matinee
events.venue: Washington Improv Theater at Studio Theatre
events.description: WIT's long-form improv show 'Escape' — an audience-driven, spontaneous performance by Washington Improv Theater's experienced ensembles at Studio Theatre on 14th St. All-ages welcome.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Stacey Axler, Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman & More (Sunday)
events.description: Final day of the Jul 9–12 showcase featuring Stacey Axler, Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman, and Ty Davis at DC Improv.

events.title: USBG Summer Evening Hours with Live Music
events.description: The US Botanic Garden hosts special extended evening hours on the third Thursday of each summer month (June 18, July 16, Aug. 20, Sept. 17). The July 16 session features Night Hawk, an American indie rock band with violin, finger-style guitar, and indie/alternative rock energy. Explore the America's State Flowers exhibit at golden hour while enjoying live music. Lemonade, botanical mocktails, snacks, and ice cream available for purchase. Free entry; no tickets required.

events.title: Lewis Belt – DC Improv Lounge Showroom (Friday)
events.description: Oakland-born stand-up comedian, actor, and producer Lewis Belt performs in DC Improv's intimate 60-seat Lounge Showroom. Part of a multi-show run through the weekend of Jul 18.

events.title: Rich Vos – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse (Friday)
events.description: Veteran stand-up comedian Rich Vos headlines at Arlington Drafthouse. Part of a multi-show run at this dine-in theater on Columbia Pike.

events.title: Audrey Stewart – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse
events.description: LA-based stand-up comedian and actress Audrey Stewart headlines at Arlington Drafthouse. A regular at the Hollywood Improv and Laugh Factory, performing her stand-up set in a full dine-in environment.

events.title: Chris D'Elia – DC Improv (Saturday)
events.description: Nationally touring headliner Chris D'Elia performs at DC Improv as part of the Jul 17–19 engagement. D'Elia is known for his popular podcast 'Congratulations.'

events.title: Chris D'Elia – DC Improv (Sunday)
events.description: Final night of Chris D'Elia's Jul 17–19 engagement at DC Improv. Check the venue for specific showtime and ticket availability.

events.title: Sheryl Underwood – DC Improv (Saturday)
events.description: Emmy Award-winning comedian and co-host of 'The Talk' Sheryl Underwood headlines DC Improv as part of her Jul 24–26 engagement.

events.title: Sheryl Underwood – DC Improv (Sunday)
events.description: Final night of Sheryl Underwood's Jul 24–26 run at DC Improv. Underwood is the Emmy-winning co-host of the CBS daytime show 'The Talk.'
